title: Avoid public static mutable fields
description:
There is no good reason to have a mutable object as the public (by default), static member of an interface. Such variables should be moved into classes and their visibility lowered.

Similarly, mutable static members of classes and enumerations which are accessed directly, rather than through getters and setters, should be protected to the degree possible.
recommendation: Move mutable fields into classes and lower their visibility.

checks.message: Move mutable fields into classes and lower their visibility.
